title: Travelers Draws Wave of Bears as Stock Eyes Best Year Since 2000
summary: The best year for Travelers Companies Inc. shares in more than 25 years has unleashed a bevy of downgrades as Wall Street bets that the stock’s gains have peaked.

title: Stocks Hold Steady as Brent Oil Falls Below $100: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street is wrapping up a jittery week with stocks holding relatively steady as oil fell after a surge that was triggered by an escalation of Middle East hostilities.

title: Global Bonds Are Reeling as Oil Surge Rekindles Inflation Threat
summary: Global bonds were pummeled this week by the latest resurgence in energy prices, delivering losses to investors who bet the worst of this year’s rout was over and teeing up credibility tests for central bankers.

title: Gold Miner Cadillac, Backers Raise C$385 Million in Canada IPO
summary: Cadillac Mines Corp. and some of its backers raised C$385 million ($273 million) in an upsized initial public offering, adding to the mining-driven rebound of listing activity in Canada.

title: BlackRock Kicks Off $12.3 Billion Bond Sale for Meta Data Center
summary: BlackRock Inc. began marketing $12.3 billion of high-grade bonds to fund a Meta Platforms Inc. data center project, testing investor appetite amid growing concerns about excessive AI infrastructure spending.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 24
summary: Brent crude retreated after hitting $100 a barrel amid concerns over the economic impact of high prices and renewed US trade tariffs, yet remained set for a substantial weekly increase as hostilities in the Middle East continue to threaten supplies.

title: Gilts Caught Between Twin Risks From Burnham Debut and Oil Shock
summary: Britain’s bond traders have spent Andy Burnham’s first week in office trying to price two different forces: the spending ambitions of his new government and the impact of oil prices hovering near $100 a barrel.

title: ‘Ridiculously’ Cheap US Bank Capital Risks Trapping Investors
summary: US banks are raising regulatory capital at the tightest valuations since the financial crisis, unsettling the most seasoned investors.

title: S&P 500 Futures Steady as Big Tech Rebounds, Oil Dips Below $100

title: Gulf States Set for Debt Binge on Routes to Avoid Hormuz
summary: Traders are preparing for a spike in borrowing from Persian Gulf nations as the region’s oil exporters seek financing to build costly bypasses around the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Metals Fluctuate as Middle East Escalation Keeps Bulls in Check

title: Momentum Crash Hits YOLO Traders’ Returns by Most in Four Years
summary: A rapid selloff in the high-flying momentum trade is wrecking the strategy’s staunchest bulls: Retail traders.

title: Oil Keeps Flowing From Red Sea as Hormuz Bypass Stays Open
summary: Millions of barrels of Saudi Arabian crude are still being shipped from the nation’s Red Sea coast to the global market, despite threats and attacks by Yemen’s Houthi militants against the kingdom’s shipping that have the potential to further destabilize the global oil trade.

title: Oil Tankers Run The Houthi Gauntlet to Keep Saudi Exports Flowing
summary: Attacks on ships by the Yemeni rebels have slowed but not stopped Red Sea oil shipments

title: Yen’s Worst Week Since May Brings It Close to 165 Versus Dollar

title: Gold Edges Up as Traders Weigh Mideast Conflict, Fed Rate Path
summary: Gold edged higher, holding firmly above the key resistance point of $4,000 an ounce even as the widening conflict in the Middle East fuels expectations for tighter monetary policy.

title: Dangote Targets $100 Billion Empire With Oil and Fertilizers
summary: Aliko Dangote is betting that Africa’s largest refinery, the continent’s biggest cement producer and his fertilizer businesses will expand his empire fivefold to $100 billion by the end of the decade.

title: Wheat Extends July Rally as War Risks Stoke Fears Over Supply
summary: Wheat climbed to a two-year high in Chicago and Paris, as escalating geopolitical tensions and worsening weather in key producing regions continued to stoke supply concerns.
